metadata.title: SubnetMagic - CCNA Subnetting Question Bank
metadata.description: Extracted and verified subnetting questions from CISCO CCNA 200-301 Mock Tests
metadata.source: CISCO CCNA 200-301 Exam Mock Tests (Abound Academy)

categories.easy: CIDR conversion, subnet mask identification, host count
categories.medium: Network/broadcast address calculation, host ranges, wildcard masks
categories.hard: VLSM, subnet design, summary routes, multi-subnet planning

easy.question: What is the subnet mask for a /27 prefix?
What is the CIDR notation for subnet mask 255.255.255.240?
How many usable host addresses are available in a /30 subnet?

explanation.note: /30 leaves 2 host bits. 2^2 - 2 = 2 usable hosts. Ideal for point-to-point WAN links.

easy.question: How many usable host addresses does a /24 subnet provide?

explanation.note: 2^8 - 2 = 256 - 2 = 254 usable hosts. Subtract 1 for network address and 1 for broadcast.

easy.question: What is the subnet mask for a /29 prefix?
What CIDR prefix provides exactly 2 usable hosts per subnet — ideal for WAN point-to-point links?

explanation.note: /30 yields 2^2-2 = 2 usable host addresses. Perfect for router-to-router WAN links with zero wasted addresses.

easy.question: How many usable hosts does a /29 subnet support?

explanation.note: /29 has 3 host bits. 2^3 - 2 = 6 usable hosts.

easy.question: Which subnet mask is equivalent to /23?
How many usable hosts does a /28 subnet provide?

explanation.note: /28 has 4 host bits. 2^4 - 2 = 14 usable hosts.

easy.question: What is the subnet mask for a /26 prefix?

explanation.note: /26 has 6 host bits. Last octet network portion: 128+64=192. So 255.255.255.192.

medium.question: What is the broadcast address of the subnet 192.168.10.64/27?

explanation.note: Magic number = 256-224 = 32. Block: 64-95. Network: .64, Broadcast: .95.

medium.question: What is the network address for host IP 192.168.1.130/25?

explanation.note: Magic number = 256-128 = 128. Blocks: 0-127, 128-255. 130 falls in block 128-255. Network = .128.

medium.question: Which is the usable host range for subnet 10.0.0.0/30?

explanation.note: /30 block size = 4. Network = .0, Broadcast = .3. Usable: .1 to .2.

medium.question: A technician needs to configure a /30 subnet between two routers. Which IP and mask combination meets this requirement?

explanation.note: /30 = 255.255.255.252. This gives exactly 2 usable hosts. 10.2.1.3 falls in the 10.2.1.0/30 subnet.

medium.question: What is the broadcast address for the subnet 192.168.1.0/26?

explanation.note: Magic number = 256-192 = 64. Block: 0-63. Network = .0, Broadcast = .63.

medium.question: You are given subnet 192.168.100.0/26. Your boss asks you to use host addresses from the second available subnet. Which range do you use?

explanation.note: Block size = 64. Subnets: .0/26 (1st), .64/26 (2nd), .128/26 (3rd), .192/26 (4th). 2nd subnet usable hosts: .65-.126.

medium.question: You are allocated 172.16.32.0/19. Which range of host addresses can be used?

explanation.note: /19 block in 3rd octet: magic = 256-224=32. Block: 32-63. Network = 172.16.32.0, Broadcast = 172.16.63.255. Hosts: .32.1 to .63.254.

medium.question: Host 192.168.200.17 is in the subnet 192.168.200.0/29. Which subnet number (ordinal) does this host belong to?

explanation.note: Block size=8. Subnets: .0 (1st), .8 (2nd), .16 (3rd), .24 (4th)... Host .17 is in the .16 block → 3rd subnet.

medium.question: What is the network address for host 192.168.10.143 with mask 255.255.255.128?

explanation.note: 255.255.255.128 = /25. Block size=128. Host 143 falls in block 128-255. Network = .128.

medium.question: A company with 8 floors has ~30-40 users per floor (320 total). Which subnet mask covers all users in a single subnet?

explanation.note: /24 = 254 hosts (not enough for 320). /23 = 510 usable hosts. 255.255.254.0 is the correct mask for /23.

medium.question: What is the broadcast address of network 192.168.1.192/26?

explanation.note: Block size=64. Block 192-255. Network=.192, Broadcast=.255.

medium.question: Which wildcard mask corresponds to subnet mask 255.255.255.224 (i.e., /27)?

explanation.note: Wildcard = inverse of subnet mask. 255-224=31. So wildcard = 0.0.0.31.

medium.question: Which wildcard mask matches subnet mask 255.255.192.0?
Your boss asks you to add a wildcard mask to an ACL to block traffic from 192.168.1.4/30. What wildcard do you use?

explanation.note: /30 = mask 255.255.255.252. Wildcard = 255-252=3. So 0.0.0.3 matches the /30 subnet.
Wildcard = 255 - 224 = 31. So wildcard mask = 0.0.0.31.

medium.question: Network 10.0.0.0/9 generates which subnets? (select all that apply)

explanation.note: /9 borrows 1 bit from the 2nd octet. Block size=128. Subnets: 10.0.0.0/9 and 10.128.0.0/9.

hard.question: A corporate office has 4 floors: 25, 30, 27, and 21 users. Which subnet configuration provides the most efficient IP allocation?

explanation.note: /27 = 30 usable hosts per subnet (covers max floor of 30 users). /25 = 126 hosts total (covers 25+30+27+21=103 users). Most efficient choice.

hard.question: Your boss hands you the subnets: 192.168.100.128/27, .160/27, .192/27, .224/27. What is the best summary route?

explanation.note: Four /27 subnets starting at .128: .128, .160, .192, .224. They all fit in 192.168.100.128/25 (block .128-.255), which is the tightest summary.

hard.question: You are given 172.16.0.0. What subnet mask generates 16 subnets with 4094 hosts per subnet?

explanation.note: /20: borrow 4 bits from class B host portion → 2^4=16 subnets. Host bits = 12 → 2^12-2=4094 hosts. Mask = 255.255.240.0.

hard.question: You are handed network design 172.16.0.0/20. Which of these statements are true? (choose all correct)
options: 16 available subnets; 4094 hosts per subnet; first subnet hosts: 172.16.0.1 - 172.16.15.254
32 available subnets; 2046 hosts per subnet; first subnet hosts: 172.16.0.1 - 172.16.31.254
8 subnets; 8190 hosts per subnet; first subnet: 172.16.0.1 - 172.16.31.254
16 subnets; 1022 hosts per subnet; first subnet: 172.16.1.1 - 172.16.14.254
hard.answer: 16 available subnets; 4094 hosts per subnet; first subnet hosts: 172.16.0.1 - 172.16.15.254

explanation.note: /20 borrows 4 bits from 3rd octet. 2^4=16 subnets. 2^12-2=4094 hosts. First subnet block: 172.16.0.0-172.16.15.255. Hosts: .0.1-.15.254.

hard.question: What is true about subnet 192.168.1.0/27? (choose all that apply)

explanation.note: Class C base /24. Borrowing 3 bits → 2^3=8 subnets. Host bits=5 → 2^5-2=30 usable hosts per subnet.

hard.question: You are handed 172.16.0.0/19. What is true about this design? (choose all that apply)

explanation.note: Class B base /16. Borrowing 3 bits → 2^3=8 subnets. Host bits=13 → 2^13-2=8190 hosts per subnet.

hard.question: A new office needs 21 users added. The existing subnets use 10.10.225.0/27. Which IP and mask minimizes wasted addresses for the new subnet?

explanation.note: 21 users need at least 23 addresses. /27=30 usable hosts. Mask 255.255.255.224. Block size=32. Next subnet after .0/27 is .32/27. Network ID = 10.10.225.32.

hard.question: An ISP assigns you 192.168.100.0/26. How many host addresses are available in this block?

explanation.note: /26 has 6 host bits. 2^6-2=62 usable host addresses.

hard.question: You need to subnet 172.16.0.0/16 to create the maximum number of /20 subnets. How many subnets can you create?

explanation.note: Moving from /16 to /20 borrows 4 bits. 2^4=16 subnets. Each has 2^12-2=4094 usable hosts.

hard.question: For OSPF configuration, what network statement and wildcard advertises only the interface 192.168.1.1/24 in area 0?

explanation.note: OSPF uses wildcard masks. /24 wildcard = 0.0.0.255. Both options A and B are technically valid; A advertises the whole /24 network (most common practice).

hard.question: You need to design subnets for 4 departments: Sales (50 hosts), IT (25 hosts), HR (10 hosts), Management (5 hosts). Using VLSM on 192.168.1.0/24, what prefix gives the tightest fit for Sales?

explanation.note: /26 provides 2^6-2=62 usable hosts, which fits Sales' 50 hosts with minimal waste. /25 would give 126 hosts — oversized. VLSM allocates: Sales /26 (.0-.63), IT /27 (.64-.95), HR /28 (.96-.111), Mgmt /29 (.112-.119).

hard.question: What is the broadcast address of 10.10.10.0/19?

explanation.note: /19 in 3rd octet: magic=256-224=32. Block starting at 0: 0-31. Broadcast = 10.10.31.255.

hard.question: A router interface has address 192.168.21.1/28. What is the usable host range for this subnet?

explanation.note: /28: block size=16. Block 0-15. Network=.0, Broadcast=.15. Usable: .1-.14 (14 hosts).
